title: calcearia umbrosa
intro: Calcearia umbrosa, also known as Wood Sorrel or Umbrella Plant, is a perennial plant with edible leaves, stems, and flowers that are native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Calcearia umbrosa include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of various conditions such as diarrhea, ulcers, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Calcearia umbrosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicine, as well as to add flavor and nutrition to salads and other dishes.
